raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I purchased gasoline at the XXXX in XXXX XXXX, NJ. I have a XXXX XXXX XXXX that holds XXXX gallons of gasoline. The price per gallon was XXXX. The maximum amount I could have spent was {$68.00}. 

The attendant started filling my car but for some reason stopped with my gas gauge showing slightly before XXXX full. I told the attendant that the tank was not full. He than requested my card to swiped it and continue filling the tank. I asked for a receipt for the first charge but he said he could not provide one since he had swiped my card again. After filling the tank, he provided me with the receipt for the second filling which totaled {$42.00}. The two charges total {$64.00}. ( {$22.00} and {$42.00}. These charges were initially listed on my Costco Visa Credit card account in the order {$22.00}, {$42.00} and {$40.00} ). Those two charges are correct as the vehicle had less than 1/8 of a tank of gas left. Costco has cameras that record the gas station. The response from Citi Cards states that they reviewed the response for Costco and have agreed with them. They said Costcos response was attached. It wasn't. Citi Cards have reversed the credit on my account. Bottom line, my card was charged an additional {$40.00} for gas that I did not receive! Costco has all the information to review their cameras and confirm what I am stating. Thanks for your assistance in the matter. A copy of this was also provided to Citi Cards to continue my dispute.
